assertions.title: Viewport scrolls to keep cursor visible

steps.action: Create 20 entries in temp dir, set LAB_HEIGHT=12 (7 body rows)
steps.observed: 20 entries created

steps.action: Run --and-exit --and-keys='DOWN x15' to move cursor past visible area
steps.observed: Viewport shows entries 11-05, cursor on entry05 (index 15) with → arrow

steps.action: Verify selected entry is visible in output
steps.observed: entry05 visible at bottom of viewport with → indicator, entries above it also visible

steps.action: Scroll down 10 times with DOWN keys
steps.observed: Keys processed

steps.expected: Scroll offset resets to 0, first filtered entry visible
steps.observed: Search shows 'e', first filtered entry (entry20) selected with → at top of list

steps.expected: Header, body with metadata, footer all visible
steps.observed: 24 lines: header (title 'Lab Directory Selection' + separator + search), 19 body entries with timestamps ('just now') and scores ('5.0'), footer (separator + keybinds)
Each entry shows 'just now, 5.0' right-aligned
Exit code 1, UI renders. Names fit at 30 cols (short names). Metadata truncated to 'w, 5.0'. Footer truncated to partial keybinds.
Exit code 1, UI renders. Names truncated with … (e.g., '2025-09-01-en…'). Title truncated. Footer truncated. No metadata shown (no space). No crash.

assertions.title: Short terminal (8-12 rows) shows minimal UI

steps.expected: Header + footer + minimum 3 body rows, no crash
steps.observed: 8 lines total: 3 header (title, separator, search) + 3 body (entry20, entry19, entry18) + 2 footer (separator, keybinds). Exactly 3 body rows = minimum met.
Entry created in test dir
Name shown as '2025-06-15-this-is-a-very-long-di…' with Unicode ellipsis at truncation point

steps.action: Run --and-exit, capture stdout and stderr separately
steps.expected: stderr has frame content, stdout empty, exit code 1
steps.observed: stderr: 3054 bytes (full TUI frame with header, entries, footer). stdout: 0 bytes. Exit code: 1.

steps.expected: Immediate exit without waiting for input
steps.observed: Command returned immediately without blocking
Exit code 0, stdout contains cd script for entry19 (2nd entry). Selection changed by DOWN key.
Selected entry is entry20 (first entry), confirming UP key works
Exit code 1, stdout empty
Exit code 1
Raw escape sequence parsed and processed correctly
Separator line has exactly 40 ─ characters
Separator line has exactly 60 ─ characters
Separator line has exactly 100 ─ characters

steps.action: Launch binary in interactive mode via Python pty module, send ESC to exit
steps.expected: Alt screen enter sequence at start, exit sequence at end
steps.observed: \e[?1049h found at byte 0 (start of output). \e[?1049l found at byte 2986 (near end of 3000 bytes). Confirms TUI enters alt screen on start and restores on exit.

steps.expected: ALT_SCREEN_ON/OFF defined and used in TerminalSession
steps.observed: ALT_SCREEN_ON = \x1b[?1049h in TerminalSession::enter(), ALT_SCREEN_OFF = \x1b[?1049l in Drop impl

frictions.description: --and-keys without --and-exit does not produce any stderr output (TUI frames). To capture rendered frames after key injection, both --and-exit and --and-keys must be combined. This wasn't immediately obvious from the testing docs.
frictions.resolution: Used --and-exit --and-keys='...' combination for all frame-capture tests

frictions.description: Alt screen buffer sequences (\e[?1049h/l) are only emitted in interactive mode, not in --and-exit or --and-keys test modes. Required Python pty module to create a real PTY for testing.
frictions.resolution: Used Python pty module to spawn the binary in a pseudo-terminal and capture raw output including alt screen sequences

summary: Tested 10 assertions: all 10 passed. TUI correctly handles viewport scrolling (VAL-TUI-019), scroll reset on filter change (VAL-TUI-020), standard 80x24 rendering (VAL-TUI-021), narrow terminal degradation with ellipsis (VAL-TUI-022), short terminal with minimum 3 body rows (VAL-TUI-023), long name truncation with … (VAL-TUI-028), --and-exit rendering (VAL-TUI-030), --and-keys injection with symbolic and raw formats (VAL-TUI-031), LAB_WIDTH/LAB_HEIGHT dimension overrides (VAL-TUI-032), and alternate screen buffer usage (VAL-TUI-033).
